titleStmt.title: SII 28.261: original edition by J. Sundaram
No. 261. (A.R. No. 415 of 1913.)

summary.p: Kāmēśvara temple, maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine, south wall.

p.hi: Tribhuvanachakravartti Rājarāja (III) Year 14, Rishabha, ba. 7, Monday, Aviṭṭam-1230 A.D., May 6,

summary.p: Records that Nāchchi, younger sister of Araiśan Toṇḍaimān endowed 3000 aṉṟḍu-naṟkāśu for the daily bathing in the river, flowers of 4 nāḻi, tiruppariśaṭṭam (apparel for the God), five sandhi lamps (lamp to be burnt at twilight), oil for śanippaḍi (oil bath on Saturdays ?) for unguents (kāppu) and the worship (pūjai-ppēṟu) of Nambiyār and another 12,000 aṉṟāḍu-naṟkāśu for six nāḻi of rice measured by āṟai-nāyakaṉ-nāḻi (per day ?) for food offerings as also for the accompanying side-dishes and for special worship and on special occasions like Vishu-saṅkramaṇa for God Kāriyāṉ-Īśvaran-uḍaiya-nāyaṉār set up at the north-western corner of the temple of Tirukkāmīśvaramuḍaiya-nāyaṉār in Āṟagaḷūr. The amount deposited was taken over by śrī-māhēśvara-kkaṇkāṇiśeyvār, dēvarkanmi and accountants (kaṇakkar) of the temple of Tirukkāmīśvaramuḍaiya-nāyaṉār with an undertaking to carry out the specified benefactions.
Finally the record states that the weight of the vessels (parikalam) and lampstand (kāl) donated by the same Nāchchi was 150 palam.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . tripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīrājarājatevaṟku yāṇṭu 10 4
Iṣapanāyaṟṟu Aparapakṣattu saptami titi tiṅkaṭkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa Aviṭṭa
ttunāḷ Āṟakaḷūruṭaiyār tirukāmiśvaramuṭaiya nāyaṉār koyiṟ śrīmāheśva
rak kaṇkāṇi ceyvārkaḷun tevarkaṉmi koyiṟ kaṇakkum Ivvūril Araican to
ṇṭaimān taṅkai nācci pakkal Upaiyaṅkoṇṭa paricāvatu Iṉṉāyiṉār koyilil mutaṟ tiru
māḷikaiyil vaṭamelai mūlaiyil praśriyamāka Eḻuntaruḷiṉa Uṭaiyār kāriyāṉ Īśvaramuṭaiya nāyaṉā
ṟku nāḷ Oṉṟukku Āṟṟiṟ tirumañcaṉaṅ kalattukkun tiruppaḷḷittāmam nāṉāḻikkuñ cāttiyaruḷat tiru
pparicaṭṭattukkuñ cantit tiruviḷakku Aiñcukkuñ caṉippaṭikku Eṇṇaikkuṅ kāppukkun nampiyāṟkup
pūcaippeṟṟukkum Iṟṟai nāḷ Oṭukkiṉa 3 1000 kācu Ikkācu muvāyiramun tiruamutukku Āṟai nāyaka
ṉ nāḻiyāl Arici Aṟunāḻikkuṅ kaṟiyamutu Aṟupalattukkum Uppamutu miḷakamutu neyyamu
tu Aṭaikkāyamutu Uḷḷiṭṭa veñcaṉattukkum viṣuveṉa sakraṅma muḷḷiṭṭa viśeṣa
pūcaikkum Iṟṟaināḷ Oṭukkiṉa kācu 10000 2 1000 Ikkācu paṉṉirāyiramum Āka Aṉṟāṭu
naṟkācu 10000 5 1000 Ikkācu patiṉaiyyāyīramum Iṉṉācci Uṭaiyār tirukkāmiśvaramu
ṭaiya nāyaṉār cipaṇṭārattile Iṟṟai nāḷ Oṭukkiṉamaiyil Innāyaṉār cipaṇṭā
rattu niṉṟum puṟappaṭṭu Iṉṉimantakkaṭṭuc cantirātittavarai Ikkāriyāṉa Īśva
ramuṭaiya nāyaṉāṟkup pūcai celuttak kaṭavatāka Ittoṇṭaimāṉ taṅkai nā
ccikku Upaiyaṅ kuṭuttuk kalveṭṭik kuṭuttoñ cimāheśvarak kaṇkāṇi cey
vārkaḷun tevakaṉmi koyiṟ kaṇakkarum Ivaṉaivom Itu paṉmāheśvara rakṣai .
Itu Aḻivu ceyvāṉ gaṅṅkaiyiṭai kumari Iṭai ceytār ceyta mahāpāpaṅ ko
ḷvār śivamastuḥ § Ikkāriyāṉaīśvaramuṭaiya nāyaṉāṟku Iṉṉācci Iṭṭa parikalamuṅ kālum
Eṭai nūṟṟaimpatiṉ palam ḥ §
Digital edition of SII 28.261 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
